subject: Update dmi-registry yang to support cmHandle State
commit_message:
Update dmi-registry yang to support cmHandle State

- Added dmi-registry@2022-02-10 which contains the new dmi-registry yang model to support the cmHandle State.
- Added changelog 12 which removes previous dmi-registries from the db
- Added changelog 13 which adds new dmi-registry
- Edited yangResourceCsvGenerator.py so that it generates for each argument given and does not generate the module_name and revision for dmi-registry@2021-12-13.
- Updated rollback for changeset 11.1

Issue-ID: CPS-874
Signed-off-by: lukegleeson <luke.gleeson@est.tech>
Change-Id: I4ab949f0bc00a52376b42a5ac8a33d397eefcedf

I tested it manually by looking at how the data changes in the table during rollbacks. Hard to have structured testing on it

Patch Set 15:

Good to me.

Here is data before this change:

  schema_set:

    id|name                   |dataspace_id|
    --|-----------------------|------------|
     1|cps-ran-schema-model   |           1|
     2|ncmp-dmi-registry-model|           2|

  yang_resource:

    id|name                                |module_name      |revision  |
    --|------------------------------------|-----------------|----------|
     1|ietf-inet-types.yang                |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     2|cps-ran-schema-model@2021-01-28.yang|dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     3|ietf-yang-types.yang                |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     4|dmi-registry@2021-05-20.yang        |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     5|dmi-registry@2021-10-20.yang        |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     6|dmi-registry@2021-12-13.yang        |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|

  schema_set_yang_resources:

    schema_set_id|yang_resource_id|
    -------------|----------------|
                1|               1|
                1|               2|
                1|               3|
                2|               4|
                2|               5|
                2|               6|


Data after this upgrade becomes:

  yang_resource:

    id|name                                |module_name      |revision  |
    --|------------------------------------|-----------------|----------|
     1|ietf-inet-types.yang                |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     2|cps-ran-schema-model@2021-01-28.yang|dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     3|ietf-yang-types.yang                |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     7|dmi-registry@2022-02-10.yang        |dmi-registry     |2022-02-10|

  schema_set_yang_resources:

    schema_set_id|yang_resource_id|
    -------------|----------------|
                1|               1|
                1|               2|
                1|               3|
                2|               7|


And when this change is rollbacked data is reverted back to:

  yang_resource:

    id|name                                |module_name      |revision  |
    --|------------------------------------|-----------------|----------|
     1|ietf-inet-types.yang                |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     2|cps-ran-schema-model@2021-01-28.yang|dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     3|ietf-yang-types.yang                |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     8|dmi-registry@2021-12-13.yang        |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
     9|dmi-registry@2021-10-20.yang        |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|
    10|dmi-registry@2021-05-20.yang        |dummy_module_name|2021-08-04|

  schema_set_yang_resources:

    schema_set_id|yang_resource_id|
    -------------|----------------|
                1|               1|
                1|               2|
                1|               3|
                2|               8|
                2|               9|
                2|              10|

From a model perspective I'm just wondering about having multiple yang resources with the exact same revision and name (dummy_module_name, 2021-08-04) but this is not part of this change anyway and we can re-discuss.
Change has been successfully merged by Bruno Sakoto
